hook: Meta is spending $135 billion on AI this year. And cutting 15,000 humans to pay for it.
headline: Meta Spends $135B on AI While Cutting 15,000 Jobs
body: Meta's 2026 AI capital expenditure projected at $115B to $135B
Up to 20% of workforce could be affected
insight: This is the clearest signal yet that Big Tech sees AI as a direct replacement for human workers, not a supplement. When a company doubles its AI budget while cutting a fifth of its people, the message is unmistakable.
caption:
Read this number twice.

$135,000,000,000.

That's what Meta plans to spend on AI infrastructure in 2026. Nearly double last year.

Now read this number: 15,000.

That's how many human jobs could be cut to help pay for it. Up to 20% of Meta's entire workforce.

Reality Labs already lost 1,500 positions. Mid level managers, QA teams, customer support, and internal IT are next.

Meta's stock climbed 3% on the layoff news. Wall Street rewarded the cuts.

This isn't about efficiency. It's about replacement. The largest social media company on Earth is betting that AI infrastructure is worth more than the people who built it.

The AI gold rush has a human cost. And we're watching it happen in real time.

#Meta #AI #Layoffs #BigTech #FutureOfWork
